Package it.polimi.ingsfw.ingsfwproject.View.GUI

Class WaitingController

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsfw.ingsfwproject.View.GUI.GUIController
it.polimi.ingsfw.ingsfwproject.View.GUI.WaitingController
public class WaitingController extends GUIController
The controller of the scene where players wait for the game to start

    • start

      public void start(javafx.stage.Stage stage) throws Exception
      Initializes and displays the "Waiting" screen.
      Specified by:
      start in class GUIController
      Parameters:
      stage - the primary stage for this application
      Throws:
      Exception - if an error occurs during initialization or scene setting

      public javafx.scene.control.TextArea getNewPlayerJoined()
      Returns the TextArea used for displaying new player join messages.

      the TextArea for new player join messages

    • addNickname

      public void addNickname(String nickname)
      Appends a new player's nickname to the TextArea for displaying join messages.
      Parameters:
      nickname - the nickname of the player who joined the game

    • setPlayerNickname

      public void setPlayerNickname(String playerNickname)
      Sets the player's nickname in a text field.
      Parameters:
      playerNickname - the nickname of the player to display

    • addMessageToChat

      public void addMessageToChat(ChatMessage message)
      Adds a message to the appropriate chat and updates the current chat display if necessary.
      Parameters:
      message - the ChatMessage to be added to the chat

    • sendMessage

      public void sendMessage(javafx.event.ActionEvent event) throws IOException
      Sends a chat message based on the user's input and updates the chat.
      Parameters:
      event - the ActionEvent triggered by the user action
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while sending the message

    • toggleChatMenu

      public void toggleChatMenu(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent)
      Toggles the visibility of the chat pane and updates the button text accordingly.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - the ActionEvent triggered by the user action
